features.featureTitle: Replace the current square grid cells in BattleBoard.tsx and GridTile.tsx with regular hexagons (pointy-top or flat-top orientation). Implement a proper hex grid layout for the 7×4 (player) and 7×4 (enemy) sections using offset or axial coordinates. Hexagon tiles should use CSS clip-path or SVG polygon to render the correct six-sided shape. Unit sprites, HP bars, and drag-and-drop logic must be adapted to work on the hexagonal grid. Gaps between hex tiles should use standard hex grid spacing so tiles tessellate correctly.

Overhaul the overall visual presentation of the game inspired by the Iron Command (钢铁命令) real-time strategy aesthetic: a gritty, high-contrast war-room look with sharp panel edges, tactical overlay styling, and a darker base palette (deep desert night tones — dark sand, aged bronze, deep lapis, ember gold) contrasted by bright unit highlight colors. The battle board should feel like a tactical command map: add a subtle grid coordinate label system (column letters, row numbers) along the board edges, a dark vignette border around the board, and unit tokens that look like military unit markers with bold iconography. The HUD should be redesigned to resemble a tactical command interface with segmented stat blocks (HP, Gold, Round, Streak) displayed in sharp bordered panels. Shop cards should adopt a compact military-briefing card style. All panels (SynergyPanel, Shop, Bench, HUD) should use dark backgrounds with gold and bronze accent borders. Typography for labels and stats should shift to a monospaced or condensed sans-serif for stat numbers while headings retain the Egyptian serif font.

Update the global Tailwind CSS design tokens in frontend/src/index.css and frontend/tailwind.config.js to introduce the new dark tactical palette: add CSS custom properties for deep-sand-dark, aged-bronze, ember-gold, deep-lapis-dark, and command-panel background colors. Ensure these tokens are applied consistently across all game components updated in REQ-14.

Generate a new dark tactical desert battle board background image to replace or supplement the existing desert background. The image should evoke a night-time aerial view of an Egyptian battlefield with a dark sandy terrain, subtle moonlit dune contours, faint hieroglyph map markings, and a tactical grid overlay texture. Use dimensions 1920×1080px. Also generate an updated dark command panel frame/border image for the game panels, with aged bronze and ember-gold tones, riveted corner details, and worn parchment texture. Use dimensions 960×640px.
